description: Directory name from filesystem used in path construction without validation; attacker can create directory named '..' to traverse outside importDir.
remediation: Validate directory names against path traversal sequences using filepath.Clean + realpath containment check before path construction.
exploitation_path: Attacker creates directory `importDir/../malicious` → os.ReadDir returns entry with Name() = '..' → path.Join(importDir, '..') escapes intended directory → findDirs reads from parent directory.

description: File opened with path constructed from unvalidated filesystem directory and file names; enables arbitrary file read.
remediation: Resolve all path components with filepath.Clean, verify the resolved path starts with importDir using strings.HasPrefix after both are passed through filepath.Abs.
exploitation_path: Directory/file names containing '..' sequences → path.Join constructs path outside importDir → os.Open reads arbitrary files → content imported into database.

description: Database fields user.Username and book.Slug used in path construction without sanitization; database compromise allows arbitrary file write.
remediation: Validate Username and Slug against path traversal patterns (reject if contains '..' or path separators) or use filepath.Clean + realpath containment check.
exploitation_path: Attacker with database write access sets Username='../..' → path.Join(exportDir, '../..', slug) writes files outside exportDir → arbitrary file overwrite.

description: Database fields user.Username and book.Slug used in filepath.Join without sanitization in V1 export; enables arbitrary file write.
remediation: Validate Username and Slug against path traversal patterns or use filepath.Clean + realpath containment check before path operations.
exploitation_path: Attacker with database write access sets Username='../../tmp' → filepath.Join(exportDir, '../../tmp', slug) writes to /tmp → arbitrary file write.

description: Asset filename validation incomplete; filepath.Base returns '..' when asset.Name is '..', bypassing the check for '.' and '/' only.
remediation: Add '..' to the disallowed filename check: if assetFileName == '/' || assetFileName == '.' || assetFileName == '..' || strings.Contains(assetFileName, string(filepath.Separator)).
exploitation_path: Attacker sets asset.Name to '..' in database → filepath.Base('..') returns '..' → check at line 308 only blocks '.' and '/' → filepath.Join(noteDir, '..') writes file to parent directory.

description: Username (online identifier PII) and userID logged together in application logs without hashing; pattern repeats throughout file at lines 102, 172, 177, 201, 259, 267, 296.
remediation: Log salted hash of username instead of plaintext, or use an opaque correlation ID that maps to username only in access-controlled audit tables.
